baseline.subject: Add Mira Core Phase 40 runtime mode kill-switch reference validation
baseline.baseline_boundary: Phases 1-40 are committed as local read-only, proposal-only, or validation-only Mira Core artifacts. No UI implementation, browser/window/capture access, runtime, server, listener, route, network, database, store, file migration, queue, lease, auth, key, secret, local execution, shell, PTY, control execution, reporting sink, send, deploy, trade, output-file, durable memory/profile commit, export, delete, restore, or raw-content storage behavior exists at this baseline.

purpose: Refresh runtime mode and kill-switch status/gap truth after committed Phase40 at def3f3b.
Pin phase inventory, schema registry, and CLI registry coverage through Phases 1-40.
Record that the Phase40 Tier0 recommendation phase41-runtime-mode-kill-switch-reference-validator is satisfied by def3f3b and must not be repeated as open work.
Select Phase40 Tier1 recommendation phase41-runtime-mode-kill-switch-status-gap-refresh-contract as fixture-only work.
Preserve Phase35, Phase36, Phase37, Phase38, Phase39, and Phase40 as current validation truth while keeping stale Phase13, Phase23, and Phase31 truth intact.
Re-state that runtime mode is disabled/default-off, local/dev-only, reference-only, unimplemented, no flag reader, and non-authorizing.
Re-state that the kill switch is visible, fail-closed, reference-only, unwired, unimplemented, and non-authorizing.
Keep runtime-mode-flag and live-kill-switch gaps unsatisfied, blocking runtime/dry-run now, reference-only, and non-authorizing.
nonGoals: No browser, window, screenshot, OCR, or capture access.
No runtime implementation or runtime start.
No runtime mode flag reader implementation or flag read.
No kill-switch wiring, execution, or live check.
No rollback execution, reporting sink, store, queue, lease, or output-file behavior.
No server, listener, route, HTTP, WebSocket, or network behavior.
No database, store, file migration, auth, token, session, signing, key, encryption, KMS, env secret, or secret material behavior.
No local execution, shell, PTY, send, deploy, trade, financial action, export, delete, restore, memory/profile commit, or raw-content storage behavior.

No claim that runtime mode status, kill-switch status, status/gap labels, socket connection, delivery acceptance, commit status, or this fixture proves runtime availability, model processing, bridge green, local-arm execution, control execution, flag reading, kill-switch wiring, or runner execution.

sourceRefsExpected.purpose: runtime mode flag, kill-switch, rollback, and dry-run control reference source
preflight proof boundary and unsatisfied gate source
disabled local dry-run implementation contract-only boundary source
ORACLE #156 disabled local dry-run readiness gap source
ORACLE #161 runtime mode and kill-switch reference source

future_green_requires: actual local flag reader implementation in a later scoped phase
actual kill-switch check wired into a later disabled dry-run control path
tests proving disabled switch blocks any runtime/dry-run path

staticValidationRules.description: Status/gap refresh baseline_commit must equal def3f3b.
Phase40 must be marked current and committed at def3f3b.
Phase35 runtime next-action truth must remain current.
Phase36 operator UI surface truth must remain current.
Phase37 control/reporting reconciliation truth must remain current.
Phase38 runtime readiness refresh truth must remain current.
Phase39 disabled local dry-run readiness gap truth must remain current.
Phase inventory must cover exactly committed Phases 1-40.
Schema registry must cover exactly 40 phases.
CLI registry must cover exactly 40 phases.
Commit chain must include Phase 1-13 package plus Phase 14-40 commits in order and end at def3f3b.
Phase40 Tier1 status/gap refresh recommendation must be selected as contract-only work.
Phase40 Tier0 mode/kill-switch reference validator recommendation must be satisfied by def3f3b and not reopened.
Phase34 prior recommendations remain satisfied and closed.
Prior stale Phase13 readiness truth must be preserved.
Prior stale Phase23 readiness truth must be preserved.
Phase31 runtime milestone refresh must remain stale/superseded by Phase34.
Source refs must include Phase26/27/29/30/32/33/37/38/39/40 artifacts.
Recent Phase36/37/38/39/40 fixture/module/CLI/test paths must be named for future reference checks.
Runtime mode status must be disabled, local/dev only, reference-only, unimplemented, no flag read, and non-authorizing.
Kill-switch status must be visible, fail-closed, reference-only, unwired, unimplemented, no live check, and non-authorizing.
Status gap matrix must include exactly runtime mode flag implementation and live kill-switch wiring, both unsatisfied and blocking.
Status/gap refresh records may describe current truth but must not mark either readiness gap satisfied now.
Refresh summary must keep runtime, dry-run, flag-read, and kill-switch wiring unauthorized now.
runtimeStarted, runnerExecuted, runtimeAvailable, serverCanExecuteLocal, serverCanProveModelProcessing, and direct Builder/Oracle targets must stay false.
Socket, delivery acceptance, Phase40 commit, mode status, kill-switch status, and status/gap refresh cannot become proof or authorization.
All no-side-effect truth fields must remain true.
Phase41 Oracle contract must not include module, CLI, test, runtime, UI, flag-read, kill-switch wiring, reporting sink, or output-file behavior.
No raw/private/terminal/screenshot/browser/secret/customer-private content may be included.
Next recommendations must be Tier0/Tier1 only and non-authorizing.
Recommendations and narrative fields must reject customer-send/deploy/trade/file-write/store-write/local-execution/control-execution/flag-read/kill-switch-wire/reporting-sink drift.
Validation report must include required literal checks and bind their ok values to recomputed fixture-required literals.
Validation report static, acceptance, tamper, required literal, and referenced path results must match fixture IDs/counts and expected failures.
Equivalent manifest inputs produce stable idempotency; phase registry, mode status, kill-switch status, gap matrix, source recommendation, closure, baseline, or commit changes alter idempotency.

forbiddenOutputSubstrings: status gap refresh authorizes dry run

nextRecommendationExpectedCandidates.action: implement validation-only runtime mode / kill-switch status-gap refresh report against this fixture
nextRecommendationExpectedCandidates.why_safe: data-only status/gap validation for committed Phases 1-40; no module beyond validator/report, no UI rendering, browser/window/capture, runtime, network, store, queue, lease, control execution, reporting sink, send, deploy, trade, flag read, kill-switch wiring, or output-file behavior

nextRecommendationExpectedCandidates.action: create fixture-only prerequisite boundary contract before any later runtime mode flag reader or kill-switch wiring slice
nextRecommendationExpectedCandidates.why_safe: contract-only prerequisite work that keeps runtime mode disabled, kill switch unwired, runtime/dry-run unavailable, all side effects blocked, and proof boundaries false before any implementation proposal is considered

summaryCriteria: Fixture remains the only Phase41 file created by Oracle.
Phase inventory, schema registry, and CLI registry requirements cover exactly Phases 1-40.
Commit chain contains Phase 1-13 package plus Phase 14-40 scoped commits and ends at def3f3b.
Source recommendation phase41-runtime-mode-kill-switch-status-gap-refresh-contract is selected as Tier1 fixture-only work.
Phase40 Tier0 recommendation phase41-runtime-mode-kill-switch-reference-validator is satisfied by def3f3b and not reopened.
Phase35, Phase36, Phase37, Phase38, Phase39, and Phase40 are current; stale Phase13, Phase23, and Phase31 truth is preserved.
Phase34 prior recommendations remain satisfied and not reopened.
ORACLE #115, ORACLE #123, ORACLE #127, ORACLE #131, ORACLE #134, ORACLE #137, ORACLE #141, ORACLE #148, ORACLE #149, ORACLE #156, and ORACLE #161 closures are explicit.
Source refs include Phase26, Phase27, Phase29, Phase30, Phase32, Phase33, Phase37, Phase38, Phase39, and Phase40 artifacts.
Runtime mode status remains disabled, local/dev only, reference-only, unimplemented, no flag reader/read, and non-authorizing.
Kill-switch status remains visible, fail-closed, reference-only, unwired, unimplemented, no live check, and non-authorizing.
Status gap matrix includes exactly runtime mode flag implementation and live kill-switch wiring, both unsatisfied, blocking runtime/dry-run now, and non-authorizing.

Capability and proof boundaries keep runtimeStarted=false, runnerExecuted=false, runtimeAvailable=false, serverCanExecuteLocal=false, serverCanProveModelProcessing=false, direct Builder/Oracle targets blocked, and proof boundaries false.

All side-effect truth fields preserve no module/CLI/test implementation, UI/runtime/server/listener/network/db/store/file migration/queue/lease/auth/key/secret/local execution/shell/PTY/browser/window/capture/control execution/reporting sink/send/deploy/trade/flag-read/kill-switch-wiring/output-file behavior.

Next recommendations are Tier0/Tier1 only, non-authorizing, and do not recommend live UI, browser/window/capture access, live runtime, customer send, deploy, trade, output files, store writes, queue/lease creation, control execution, reporting sink, flag read, kill-switch wiring, or local execution.
